title: symphytum × mosquense
intro: Symphytum × mosquense, also known as Russian comfrey, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae) and is a cross between Symphytum ibericum and Symphytum asperum. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine for treating wounds, burns, and other skin irritations due to its high concentration of allantoin, a compound that promotes tissue repair and reduces inflammation. The health benefits of Russian comfrey include its ability to accelerate wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and even support bone health by promoting calcium absorption. Russian comfrey can be prepared as a herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health.
